title: gmelina peltata
intro: Gmelina peltata, also known as the Kamani tree, is a species of flowering plant in the family Verbenaceae. It is a large, evergreen tree native to the tropical regions of Asia and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Gmelina peltata include its ability to aid in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
